categories.name: Written and Oral Communication
outcomes: Construct organized, evidence-based written and oral arguments
Adapt communication to audience and context
Use appropriate citation and attribution practices

categories.name: Quantitative Literacy
outcomes: Interpret and reason with quantitative information

categories.name: Ethics
outcomes: Identify ethical dimensions of personal, professional, and civic life

categories.name: Critical Thinking and Creative Expression
outcomes: Analyze and evaluate claims, evidence, and arguments
Synthesize information to produce original work

requirements.signature_assignment: Each gen-ed course must include at least one signature assignment scored on a standardized rubric and submitted to the Office of Institutional Research annually
requirements.review_cycle: Gen-ed courses reviewed every 5 years by the Gen-Ed Review Committee

constraints_for_agent: Gen-ed courses must maintain coverage of their designated category outcomes
Signature assignment format and rubric cannot be changed without committee approval
Alternative assessment formats are acceptable if they assess the same gen-ed outcomes
Reducing the number of assessed outcomes is NOT acceptable
